title: AHP Conformance — Scenario Fixture
description: One language-neutral, DECLARATIVE conformance scenario: ordered metadata + an ordered list of steps that all six client runners and the host runner load and execute identically. A scenario is data, not script: each step is a single discriminated op (mirroring the round-trip corpus's neutral `type` discriminator) so every SDK can decode it with no language-specific hints. The convergence-assertion model is snapshot-and-stream: server.* steps drive the client's reducer; client.assert.* steps check the resulting client-reduced state / events / errors. See SCHEMA.md for the prose contract and the per-op field tables.

id.description: Stable scenario id. Mirrors the behavior-id grammar (<domain>.<concept>.<scenario-class>[.<discriminator>], 3-5 dot segments of [A-Za-z0-9-]) so an independent author naturally lands on the same id for the same scenario. This IS the file's merge key; the file is named <id>.scenario.json.

behaviorIds.description: The discovery behavior-id(s) this scenario covers (cite from conformance/discovery/out/*.jsonl). Each is the same <domain>.<concept>.<scenario-class>[.<discriminator>] grammar. This is the back-link that makes the scenario discoverable from the coverage matrix and vice-versa — the convergence point between Part 1 (discovery) and Part 2 (authoring).

description.description: Human-readable one-liner: what protocol behavior this scenario proves.

protocolVersion.description: The AHP protocol version this scenario targets, e.g. '0.3.0'. Runners that negotiate a different version skip or fail the scenario per their policy.

pinClock.description: Optional scenario-level clock pin (epoch-ms). When set, the runner pins the impure clock (the host's Date.now / the client's now-provider) to this value BEFORE step 0 so impure reducer fields (e.g. summary.modifiedAt, microsoft/agent-host-protocol#186) are deterministic. A `pin.clock` step may re-pin mid-scenario. null / absent = runner default (no pin).

notes.description: Optional free-text: authoring notes, cross-references, divergence detail, known-skip reasons per client.

steps.description: Ordered list of steps. Steps execute strictly in array order. Each step is exactly one op, discriminated by the `op` field.

oneOf.title: client.request
oneOf.description: The client sends a JSON-RPC request (method+params+id) to the server. Models the client driving the protocol (e.g. initialize, subscribe). A later server.response with forId === this id supplies the reply.

params.description: JSON-RPC params (any JSON value, or absent).

id.description: JSON-RPC request id; server.response.forId must match it.

oneOf.title: server.response
oneOf.description: The server replies to an earlier client.request. Exactly one of result / error is present (JSON-RPC success vs error response). The client decodes it and (for snapshot-bearing results like initialize/subscribe) seeds its reduced state from the snapshot.

forId.description: The id of the client.request this responds to.

result.description: JSON-RPC success result (any JSON value). Mutually exclusive with `error`.

error.description: JSON-RPC error object. Mutually exclusive with `result`.

code.description: JSON-RPC error code, e.g. -32601 (method not found).

oneOf.title: server.notify
oneOf.description: The server emits a JSON-RPC notification (method+params, no id) — e.g. an 'action' envelope the host emits. The client routes it through its reducer. This is the live action stream that drives convergence.

params.description: Notification params (any JSON value). For 'action', the ActionEnvelope { channel, action, serverSeq, origin }.

oneOf.title: client.assert.state
oneOf.description: Assert the client-reduced state at a dotted path equals an expected JSON value. `path` uses the same dotted-selector convention as the round-trip corpus's `expect` keys (e.g. 'summary.title', 'lifecycle', 'turns.0.id'). Absent `path` (or '') asserts the whole reduced state equals `equals` (the byte-for-byte convergence assertion).

channel.description: Optional channel/resource URI to scope the assertion to a specific reduced channel when the runner tracks more than one (e.g. 'ahp-session:/compliant'). Absent = the scenario's single/primary channel.

path.description: Dotted selector into the reduced state, e.g. 'summary.title'. Numeric segments index arrays. Empty / absent = whole-state assertion (convergence).

equals.description: Expected JSON value at `path` (deep-equal). Any JSON value, including null.

oneOf.title: client.assert.event
oneOf.description: Assert the client observed an event/action matching the given partial shape. `matches` is a partial object deep-contained in some observed event (e.g. { type: 'session/titleChanged', title: 'Hello' }). Mirrors the round-trip corpus's partial-`expect` discipline: only the named fields must match; unnamed fields are ignored.

matches.description: Partial event shape that must be deep-contained in at least one observed event.

oneOf.title: client.assert.error
oneOf.description: Assert the client surfaced a protocol error with the given JSON-RPC code (and optionally a message substring). Used for negative / error-path scenarios (e.g. method-not-found, version-mismatch).

message.description: Optional: a substring the surfaced error message must contain.

oneOf.title: client.reconnect
oneOf.description: The client reconnects and resubscribes, optionally declaring the last server sequence it saw so the server can replay-from or resnapshot. Models reconnect-replay behavior. The following server.* steps supply the replay (delta-from-seq) or the resnapshot.

channel.description: Optional channel/resource URI being resubscribed. Absent = the scenario's primary channel.

lastSeenServerSeq.description: The highest serverSeq the client had applied before the disconnect. null / absent = the client has no cursor and expects a full resnapshot.

oneOf.title: pin.clock
oneOf.description: Pin the impure clock to a fixed epoch-ms value from this step forward, so impure reducer fields (summary.modifiedAt etc.) are deterministic across implementations. Usually used once at the top, but may re-pin mid-scenario. The scenario-level `pinClock` is the BEFORE-step-0 form; this op is the mid-stream form.

value.description: Epoch-ms the clock returns from now on, e.g. 9999.
